title: Test lesson
root_cause: Missing validation
prevention_rule: Add input checks

title: Mirror agent-context manifest updates across runtime and cached paths
root_cause: Agent-facing context fields can land in init output first while runtime scoping manifests or cached agent-context generators still omit them, leaving downstream consumers inconsistent.
prevention_rule: When adding a new agent-facing context field, update both src/lib/context.js and src/lib/codebase-intel.js in the same task and add scoped integration coverage for at least one live init path plus one cached-context path.

title: Make validate-commands raw output consumable inside executor shells
root_cause: The validator emits an @file indirection path whose target is not readable by a follow-up shell step, so executors cannot inspect pass/fail details after successful runs.
prevention_rule: For commands that emit @file indirection, provide a stable stdout/json mode or guarantee the referenced file remains readable for immediate subprocess inspection in the same shell.

title: Validate surfaced command excerpts instead of whole agent prompts
root_cause: The shared command-integrity validator scans slash-like substrings, so full agent/skill files produced false failures from config-path placeholders such as __OPENCODE_CONFIG__/bgsd-oc and non-guidance CLI snippets.
prevention_rule: When locking command guidance surfaces, feed the validator curated user-facing command excerpts (or pre-filtered guidance lines) while keeping exact-file assertions separate for full-file wording coverage.

title: execute:commit can fail on dirty trees during path-scoped plan commits
root_cause: The execute:commit helper rejects dirty worktrees even when the requested files are isolated, so phase-planning runs can fail to persist valid PLAN.md changes in active development workspaces.
prevention_rule: When commit_docs workflows are expected to commit only specific planning artifacts, either allow safe path-scoped commits on dirty trees or surface a built-in fallback recommendation before the helper aborts.

title: Planner should default to execute plans unless using the full TDD template
root_cause: The planner prompt requires a visible TDD decision for every implementation plan, but the plan-structure validator still expects legacy TDD-only sections like <feature> when frontmatter type is set to tdd. Marking plans as tdd from the decision alone creates avoidable validation failures.
prevention_rule: When a plan records '> **TDD Decision:** Selected' but does not intentionally use the dedicated TDD plan structure, keep frontmatter type as execute and reserve type:tdd for plans that include the full TDD template requirements.

title: Plan-finalization helpers should accept current STATE and REQUIREMENTS formats
root_cause: verify:state complete-plan and plan:requirements mark-complete still expect older markdown shapes, so current STATE.md and backtick-form REQUIREMENTS entries were not parsed during execution.
prevention_rule: Keep state/requirements mutators aligned with the live markdown formats used by generated planning files and add regression tests covering current repo artifacts before relying on those helpers in execute-plan flows.

title: Requirement completion helper misses backticked IDs
root_cause: plan:requirements mark-complete still matches only bold checkbox IDs, but current REQUIREMENTS.md uses backticked IDs, so automated plan-finalization leaves completed requirements unchecked.
prevention_rule: Normalize requirement checkbox parsing for both bold and backticked ID formats before relying on mark-complete during executor finalization.

title: Requirements completion CLI misses backtick-wrapped IDs
root_cause: plan:requirements mark-complete matches raw tokens, but REQUIREMENTS.md stores canonical IDs inside backticks, so execution-state automation cannot mark completed requirements through the standard command.
prevention_rule: Normalize requirement IDs by stripping optional markdown backticks before lookup so execution workflows can complete traced requirements without manual file edits.

title: State advance-plan should handle Not started phase state
root_cause: verify:state advance-plan assumes STATE.md already has numeric Current Plan and Total Plans in Phase values, but freshly planned phases can still be in 'Not started' / 'TBD' form when the first execution plan completes.
prevention_rule: Teach verify:state advance-plan to initialize numeric plan position from on-disk phase plans and summaries before incrementing so first-plan execution can follow the normal state-update protocol without manual repair.

title: Verifier helper should fail structured, not crash
root_cause: The installed verify:verify artifacts helper can crash with an unhandled ReferenceError (writeDebugDiagnostic undefined), forcing manual artifact verification and weakening verifier workflow reliability.
prevention_rule: Add regression coverage for verifier helper startup paths and return structured error JSON when debug helpers are unavailable so verification can continue or fail loudly without a stack trace.

title: Rebuild runtime before runtime-backed integration proofs
root_cause: Integration tests for CLI commands execute bin/bgsd-tools.cjs, so source-only edits can leave tests exercising stale runtime artifacts until npm run build refreshes the bundle.
prevention_rule: When a task changes source used by runtime-backed integration commands, rebuild before the first green proof instead of waiting for final verification.

title: Place Node test-name filters before file paths
root_cause: Focused verification initially still executed the entire test file because the Node test runner respected --test-name-pattern only when the flag appeared before positional file arguments in this environment.
prevention_rule: When using node --test for scoped proof, place --test-name-pattern before test file paths and verify the filtered run reports only the intended subtests before relying on it for green evidence.

title: Verify requirement marker helpers against on-disk checklist state
root_cause: The plan:requirements mark-complete helper returned not_found for phase requirement IDs during plan completion, so metadata automation did not update REQUIREMENTS.md even though the workflow expected it to.
prevention_rule: After plan:requirements mark-complete, always read REQUIREMENTS.md and repair any still-unchecked plan requirement IDs before reporting success or creating the metadata commit.

title: Verify plugin ESM import after touched plugin rebuilds
root_cause: Runtime rebuilds can pass bundle validation while still failing at module import time when plugin code pulls CLI-only CommonJS helpers into the ESM bundle.
prevention_rule: After any plan that touches src/plugin/** or plugin-adjacent runtime imports, run a direct import smoke check like node -e "import('./plugin.js')" before treating build output as verified.

title: Guard verifier helpers against missing debug logger dependencies
root_cause: The installed verify:verify artifacts path can throw ReferenceError: writeDebugDiagnostic is not defined while warming caches, which turns artifact verification into a tooling failure instead of a reportable result.
prevention_rule: Keep debug-diagnostic hooks optional in verifier/cache code paths and add a regression test that verify:verify artifacts returns structured status even when debug logging helpers are absent or disabled.

title: Plugin source tests need an explicit ESM loading path
root_cause: The local Node test runner treats src/plugin/*.js source files as non-importable CommonJS in this workspace, so new source-level plugin tests can fail before exercising behavior.
prevention_rule: When adding focused tests for new src/plugin modules, either exercise the built plugin bundle or copy the touched source files to temporary .mjs fixtures before importing them so RED/GREEN proofs reflect behavior instead of loader mismatches.

title: Executor self-check should verify execute:commit hashes against commit_id
root_cause: The execute-plan self-check example checks jj change IDs, but execute:commit returns git commit hashes. Executors can incorrectly think commits are missing unless they inspect commit_id instead.
prevention_rule: Update executor workflow and summary guidance so self-check commands verify execute:commit hashes with jj commit_id.shortest(...) or clearly state when to use change_id versus commit_id.

title: Prefer focused plugin suite patterns when file-level plugin tests hang
root_cause: The generic file-level test runner for tests/plugin.test.cjs can stall in this workspace even after touched-surface cmux assertions are green, which makes the broader gate unreliable for plan-local verification.
prevention_rule: When a plan touches only one focused plugin behavior and tests/plugin.test.cjs hangs under file-level execution, run the touched suite with --test-name-pattern plus the relevant focused test file, then keep npm run build as the broader runtime gate and document the stalled broad attempt once.

title: Prefer focused node --test slices when plugin.test hangs after partial progress
root_cause: Broad plugin test invocations can hang after unrelated suites complete, leaving verification evidence ambiguous even when the touched cmux slice is green.
prevention_rule: For phase verification, run targeted node --test file or name-pattern slices for the touched surface first, then record any broader-suite timeout separately instead of relying on the hanging aggregate command.

title: Plan checks should re-evaluate must_haves observability after pre-run analyzers
root_cause: Pre-run structure and realism analyzers can encourage checkers to assume must_haves metadata quality is already settled, but approval readiness still depends on whether truths remain user-observable and verifier-facing.
prevention_rule: Even when plan-structure and analyze-plan are reported as already run, re-check each plan's must_haves truths for user-observable wording and flag implementation-shaped truths during final approval review.

title: State completion helpers should tolerate legacy unbolded session fields
root_cause: verify:state complete-plan expected a narrower STATE.md field shape than the live repo used, so plan completion repair failed even though the needed Current Position data existed on disk.
prevention_rule: Make state-completion helpers parse both bolded and unbolded STATE.md field variants before refusing completion-path updates, then add regression coverage against live legacy formatting.

title: Invalidate parser caches before hook-driven state re-reads
root_cause: Hook-triggered refresh logic reused cached planning parser results after on-disk STATE.md changes, so lifecycle updates could publish stale sidebar metadata even when the sync helper was correct.
prevention_rule: When lifecycle hooks recompute UI or status from planning files after potential writes, invalidate parser caches for the repo before re-reading project state and rerun the focused proof against the rebuilt runtime.

title: Artifact verifier patterns should avoid comma-joined contains fields
root_cause: Plan artifact metadata used comma-joined  strings, but the artifact verifier treated each full string as one literal pattern and produced false stub-like failures despite implemented code and passing focused tests.
prevention_rule: Artifact-verification metadata should either support list semantics for multiple expected symbols or require one pattern per entry so verifier evidence matches real implementations without manual override.

title: Phase research should trace requirement IDs to linked PRDs before final recommendations
root_cause: Phase prompts often include local context and requirements, but the phase boundary and non-goals can still drift unless the researcher follows traceability from REQUIREMENTS.md to the linked PRD/backlog artifacts.
prevention_rule: After mandatory input reads, phase researchers should grep requirement traceability for the phase's requirement IDs and read the linked PRD/backlog sections before writing prescriptive recommendations.

title: Cold-cache plan-structure should not crash
root_cause: The first verify:verify plan-structure invocation can hit a cold-cache warmup path that references writeDebugDiagnostic before definition, causing a ReferenceError and forcing a retry before plan verification can proceed.
prevention_rule: Guard cache-warmup diagnostics behind a defined helper or remove the call so verifier subcommands remain deterministic on first run without manual retry.

title: Re-run plan-structure verification on revised plans
root_cause: Plan approval can hinge on verifier-consumable must_haves metadata that looks present in the file but only  confirms is acceptable.
prevention_rule: On every plan re-check, rerun  and  against the current files before carrying forward any prior blockers or approvals.

title: Repair STATE.md after complete-plan leaves stale phase header
root_cause: The verify:state complete-plan helper updated Current focus for the active plan but left the top Current Position phase line on the previous phase, so metadata success reporting would have been inconsistent without a manual readback.
prevention_rule: After every complete-plan call, read the Current focus and Current Position header together; if phase or plan disagree, repair STATE.md before the final metadata commit and flag the helper for follow-up.

title: Use stable verifier tokens in plan artifact metadata
root_cause: Plan artifact metadata used prose/comma-separated contains strings, so artifact helper checks reported false negatives even though the code and focused tests verified the implementation.
prevention_rule: When writing PLAN.md artifact metadata, use stable exact function/test identifiers or separately listed verifier tokens instead of prose lists in a single contains field.

title: Initial roadmap validation should not rely on post-execution requirement coverage
root_cause: The available requirements validation command checks whether traced phases already have summaries, which makes a freshly created roadmap look unresolved even when traceability is correct.
prevention_rule: When roadmapping, pair roadmap and state validators with a traceability-shape check that validates requirement-to-phase mappings without requiring executed phase summaries.

title: Prefer simple file-check verifies in PLAN.md
root_cause: verify:verify analyze-plan tokenizes task <verify> commands heuristically and can misread complex node -e snippets or shell substitutions as unavailable file paths, causing false approval blockers.
prevention_rule: When writing PLAN.md verification commands, prefer simple test/rg/npm commands over embedded node -e scripts or command substitutions unless the extra complexity is essential.

title: Retry verify:verify plan-structure before treating tool failures as blockers
root_cause: The first plan-structure invocation threw an internal CLI ReferenceError, but an immediate retry succeeded, showing some verifier failures can be transient rather than plan defects.
prevention_rule: When a required verifier command fails unexpectedly, retry once before converting the failure into a plan blocker; only persistent failures should be reported as unavailable validation steps.

title: Seed numeric STATE plan fields before first execute-phase run
root_cause: verify:state complete-plan assumes STATE.md already contains parseable numeric Current Plan and Total Plans in Phase values, but newly planned phases can still hold 'Not started' and 'TBD'.
prevention_rule: When phase planning finishes, normalize STATE.md to numeric execution-ready placeholders (phase number, current plan 01, total plan count) so execute-phase helpers do not fail on the first completion update.

title: Artifact verifier false-negatives on comma-separated contains metadata
root_cause: Plan artifact metadata can express multiple required markers in one comma-separated contains string, but verify:verify artifacts may treat that payload as one literal pattern and report missing sections even when the artifact clearly contains each marker.
prevention_rule: When artifact verification fails on contains metadata, inspect whether the plan encoded multiple expected markers as one comma-separated string; if so, cross-check the artifact directly and flag the helper result as a tooling warning instead of assuming the artifact is a stub.

title: Artifact verifier misreads comma-separated contains metadata
root_cause: verify:verify artifacts appears to treat comma-separated PLAN.md contains strings as a single literal pattern, so present headings/fields are reported missing during phase verification.
prevention_rule: When PLAN artifact metadata needs multiple required markers, encode them in a verifier-supported form or update the helper to split and test each marker independently before using contains results as evidence.

title: Phase researchers should fall back to apply_patch when write tool is unavailable
root_cause: The phase-researcher workflow assumes a Write-capable runtime, but some tool manifests expose read/edit/apply_patch without a direct write function.
prevention_rule: After loading context, check available filesystem mutation tools; if Write is absent, switch immediately to apply_patch for artifact creation instead of delaying at output time.

title: Retry plan-structure after cache-warm verifier crash
root_cause: The first parallel verify:verify plan-structure call can crash during cache warm-up because the bundled verifier references an undefined writeDebugDiagnostic helper before returning results.
prevention_rule: When plan-structure crashes during initial parallel verification, immediately rerun the affected plan serially and treat the failure as a tooling retry case rather than a malformed plan.

title: Avoid first-run parallel plan-structure verifier fan-out on cold cache
root_cause: Running multiple verify:verify plan-structure commands in parallel on a cold cache can trigger a bgsd-tools bug where cache warming calls an undefined writeDebugDiagnostic helper, causing a spurious verifier crash.
prevention_rule: Warm phase/plan cache with one serial verifier invocation before parallelizing additional plan-structure checks, or fix bgsd-tools so cache warming never references undefined diagnostics.

title: Summary verifier mistakes backticked commands for file references
root_cause: verify:summary extracts every backticked token that looks path-like, so TDD audit command examples in SUMMARY.md can be misclassified as missing files during self-check.
prevention_rule: Teach verify:summary to ignore command-form inline code blocks or validate only file-list sections before using it as a completion gate.

title: Record unrelated baseline failures alongside rebuilt smoke proof for shared-file TDD gates
root_cause: Plan-level TDD targets that execute whole shared test files can stay red because unrelated pre-existing failures live in the same files even after the touched behavior is fixed.
prevention_rule: When a plan's declared broad file gate stays red for unrelated baseline failures, record that baseline once, then run a rebuilt-runtime smoke proof or narrower touched-surface check before reporting completion.

title: Treat hanging broad node:test file gates as one-shot evidence attempts
root_cause: File-level node:test gates can still drag in unrelated long-running suites or pre-existing failures even after touched-surface proofs already passed, which wastes time when executors keep retrying the same broad command.
prevention_rule: Attempt the broad gate once, record the unrelated failure or hang, then switch to rebuilt-runtime plus explicit touched-surface proof commands instead of rerunning the same hanging file set.

title: State completion helper can fail after successful execution
root_cause: The bundled verify:state complete-plan path references writeDebugDiagnostic, which is undefined at runtime, so plan-finalization can fail after task work is already complete.
prevention_rule: Add a post-build smoke test for verify:state complete-plan and document the executor fallback to granular state-update commands when the helper crashes.

title: Keep docs TDD targets isolated when shared files have baseline failures
root_cause: A shared docs regression file already contained unrelated failing assertions, so the plan's broad node:test target could not provide clean GREEN proof for the new workspace-first slice.
prevention_rule: When adding TDD coverage to an existing multi-contract docs test file, ensure the new phase's checks can run in isolation or pair the broad gate with an explicit focused smoke command in the plan.

title: Verification helpers should not depend on missing debug globals
root_cause: The bundled verification path can invoke cache warm-up code that calls writeDebugDiagnostic even when that helper is undefined in the runtime bundle, causing verify:key-links to crash before returning evidence.
prevention_rule: Add a bundled-runtime smoke check that exercises verify:key-links and other cache-warming verification commands, and guard optional debug hooks behind a defined-function check before calling them.
